methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Velo3D is a public-stage company in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector that has raised over $300M in total capital. The valuation analysis draws from a narrow peer universe comprising one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. While 11 same-sector peers exist within Robotics & Manufacturing, the filtering criteria—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for reliable median triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of robotics and manufacturing subsectors and their divergent unit economics and capital requirements.

This constraint significantly limits quantitative triangulation. A defensible valuation range cannot be established from the available data without methodological compromise. Forward-looking analysis would benefit from either: (1) expanded comparable set definition that relaxes stage or tier criteria with explicit adjustment factors, (2) DCF analysis anchored to the company's capital deployment trajectory and gross margin profile, or (3) precedent transaction data from the Robotics & Manufacturing M&A market. Current data sufficiency does not support a point estimate or narrow band.
